title: External API: expose Autopilot/CoPilot (conversational + programmatic agent-building)
author: bugattive
body:
## Summary

**Autopilot / CoPilot** — the chat-based agent builder in `backend/copilot/` — is now the core of the platform, but it has **essentially no external API**. The full conversational agent-building experience is frontend-locked (internal API only). Enterprises adopting AutoGPT by integrating it into their own products cannot embed the builder, build agents from a natural-language brief programmatically, or drive a chat session over the public API.

This issue tracks exposing Autopilot on the external API surface (`/external-api/v1/...`). It builds on the hardening in **#13556** and the enterprise-readiness epic in **#13557** — those cover the plumbing (rate limiting, SDKs, docs, webhooks, idempotency); this covers exposing the **core product**.

---

## Current state (research findings)

**What Autopilot is** (`backend/copilot/`): a full **stateful** chat-based agent builder with **51 tools** in `copilot/tools/` — e.g. `create_agent`, `edit_agent`, `customize_agent`, `fix_agent`, `validate_agent`, `run_agent`, `find_agent`, `run_block`, `decompose_goal`, `agent_generator`, `agent_browser`, `e2b_sandbox`, `manage_schedules`, `manage_presets`.

**Served on the INTERNAL API only** — `/api/chat/*` (`backend/api/features/chat/routes.py`, mounted at `backend/api/rest_api.py:426`):
- Session lifecycle: `POST/GET /sessions`, `GET/DELETE /sessions/{id}`, title/pin/assign-user
- Conversational streaming: `POST /sessions/{id}/stream` (SSE), `/cancel`, pending messages
- Usage/paywall: `/usage`, `/usage/reset`, config, sharing, suggested-prompts
- Auth = JWT user (frontend), with paywall + credit + rate limiting

**What's exposed EXTERNALLY today** — only **2 of the 51 tools**, statelessly, at `/external-api/v1/tools/` (`backend/api/external/v1/tools.py`):
- `POST /tools/find-agent` and `POST /tools/run-agent`, gated by the `USE_TOOLS` scope.
- These use **ephemeral, non-persisted sessions** — no conversation, no memory, no building, no streaming. The file header explicitly notes session rate limits don't apply.

**Gap:** the entire conversational agent-building experience — the platform's core — has no external API. External consumers only get "search marketplace" and "run a marketplace agent."

---

## Proposed external API surface

New namespace: **`/external-api/v1/copilot/*`**, in three tiers.

### Tier 1 — Conversational Autopilot API (embed the builder)
Mirror the internal `/api/chat` surface for API-key consumers so an enterprise can embed the chat builder in their own product. The SSE streaming machinery already exists internally.
- [ ] `POST /copilot/sessions` — create a session (optionally bound to an existing graph)
- [ ] `POST /copilot/sessions/{id}/stream` — send a message, stream response via SSE
- [ ] `GET /copilot/sessions/{id}` / `GET /copilot/sessions/{id}/messages` — history
- [ ] `POST /copilot/sessions/{id}/cancel`, `DELETE /copilot/sessions/{id}`
- [ ] New scope: **`USE_COPILOT`** (stateful, persisted, billed)

### Tier 2 — Programmatic build API (no chat UI needed)
"Give a brief, get an agent" — for CI/CD, internal tooling, agent factories. Long-running → return a job id and pair with completion webhooks (#13557 C-7).
- [ ] `POST /copilot/agents:build` — natural-language spec -> creates/returns a graph (wraps `create_agent` / `decompose_goal` / `agent_generator`)
- [ ] `POST /copilot/agents/{graph_id}:edit` — NL edit instruction (wraps `edit_agent` / `customize_agent` / `fix_agent`)
- [ ] `POST /copilot/agents/{graph_id}:validate` — wraps `validate_agent`
- [ ] New scope: **`BUILD_AGENT`**

### Tier 3 — Selected tool endpoints (expand beyond the current 2)
Promote more of the 51 tools to stable, documented external endpoints where they stand alone: `run-block`, `list-agent-triggers`, `manage-schedules`, `manage-presets`, `setup-agent-webhook-trigger`. Keep sandbox / browser / graphiti tools **internal-only** (security + cost).

---

## Cross-cutting requirements (shared with #13556 / #13557)
- [ ] Add `USE_COPILOT`, `BUILD_AGENT` to the `APIKeyPermission` enum (`schema.prisma`).
- [ ] API-key quotas / rate limiting — the external tools file already flags that session limits don't apply (#13556 item 1).
- [ ] Async + completion webhooks + idempotency keys for builds/runs (#13557 C-7 / C-8).
- [ ] SDKs + streaming docs/examples — an embeddable chat API needs a distributed SDK (#13557 Track A/B).

---

## Why this matters / sequencing

Exposing even **Tier 1** (embeddable conversational API) or **Tier 2** (programmatic build) is the single biggest enterprise-integration lever — it exposes the *core product* rather than the plumbing around it. Recommended order: Tier 2 (simpler, request/response + webhook) or Tier 1 (higher value, needs SSE + session auth) first; Tier 3 incrementally.

## Related
- Security / robustness hardening: #13556
- Enterprise integration readiness epic: #13557

title: External API: enterprise integration readiness (docs, SDKs, self-service OAuth, webhooks, idempotency)
author: bugattive
body:
## Summary

The public **External API** (`backend/api/external/`, served under `/external-api/v1/...`) is functionally capable but **not yet integration-ready for enterprise adoption**. Enterprises increasingly adopt automation platforms by integrating them into their own services via API — so the external API is a strategic surface. Today an enterprise can *call* it, but can't easily *discover* it (no distributed reference or SDK), can't *onboard at scale* (no self-serve OAuth app registration), and can't *build reliably* on it (no completion webhooks, no idempotency, poll-only async).

This is the **epic** tracking enterprise-readiness work. It complements the security/robustness hardening tracked in **#13556** (rate limiting, `lastUsedAt`, error mapping, credential/OAuth hardening, HTTP caching, test coverage). Where relevant, items below reference #13556.

---

## Current state (research findings)

**Docs** (GitBook, under `docs/platform/`):
- Exists: `integrating/api-guide.md` (auth methods, scopes table, curl quickstarts) and `integrating/oauth-guide.md` (OAuth 2.0 + PKCE + SSO), wired into `docs/platform/SUMMARY.md`.
- Missing: no repo-hosted API reference — the endpoint reference is entirely offloaded to a live external Swagger UI (`backend.agpt.co/external-api/docs`), so self-hosters/offline readers get nothing. Broken links: `api-guide.md` links twice to `sso-guide.md`, which does not exist (actual file is `oauth-guide.md`). No "create your first API key" walkthrough, no versioning/deprecation policy, no changelog, no rate-limit/error-code reference, no Postman collection, no enterprise guide.

**SDK / clients:**
- No external SDK exists in any language. The Orval-generated client (`frontend/src/app/api/__generated__/`) targets the **internal** API only — its `openapi.json` contains zero `/external-api` references and it is bundled in the frontend, not distributed. `backend/sdk/` is the block-authoring SDK, unrelated.

**Developer / self-service surface:**
- API-key management UI exists (`frontend/src/app/(platform)/settings/api-keys/`) with scoped permissions.
- OAuth apps: users can list/toggle/logo apps (`settings/oauth-apps/`) but **cannot self-register**. New `client_id`/`client_secret` pairs are minted by an admin running a CLI (`backend/cli/oauth_tool.py`) that emits a raw SQL INSERT — a hard blocker for onboarding at scale.

**Reliability primitives:**
- No completion webhooks — consumers must poll `GET /external-api/v1/graphs/{graph_id}/executions/{graph_exec_id}/results` (`backend/api/external/v1/routes.py:262`). No SSE, no long-poll, no callback.
- No idempotency keys — `grep -i idempoten` across the backend returns nothing; retried POSTs create duplicate executions **and** duplicate credit charges.
- Input-schema discovery is partial — blocks expose schemas via `GET /external-api/v1/blocks`, but there is no external `GET /graphs/{id}` returning a graph's `input_schema`.

---

## Track A — Discoverability & Docs (fastest wins; unblocks evaluation)

- [ ] **A-1. Publish the external OpenAPI spec into the repo/docs.** Add a build step exporting `/external-api/openapi.json` and render a Redoc/Swagger reference page so the reference is versioned and available offline / for self-hosters.
- [ ] **A-2. Fix broken docs links + add "Create your first API key" walkthrough.** Repair the two `sso-guide.md` links in `api-guide.md` (target is `oauth-guide.md`); add a step-by-step page for `settings/api-keys/`.
- [ ] **A-3. Add versioning + deprecation policy + changelog docs** (pairs with #13556 item 10).
- [ ] **A-4. Ship a Postman collection / `.http` examples** generated from the external spec.

## Track B — SDKs (integration ergonomics)

- [ ] **B-5. Generate and publish standalone SDKs** from the external OpenAPI spec — Python (PyPI) + TypeScript (npm) — reusing the existing Orval / openapi-generator tooling pointed at `/external-api`. Highest-leverage item for adoption.

## Track C — Reliability & self-service primitives (deepest, highest value)

- [ ] **C-6. Self-service OAuth app registration** — `POST /api/oauth/apps` + UI in `settings/oauth-apps/`, replacing the admin-CLI-and-SQL flow (`backend/cli/oauth_tool.py`).
- [ ] **C-7. Completion webhooks** — let a consumer register a callback URL (HMAC-signed, with retries) to be notified when an execution finishes, instead of polling.
- [ ] **C-8. Idempotency keys** on execute endpoints — dedup by `Idempotency-Key` header to prevent duplicate executions/charges on retry (critical since billing is involved).
- [ ] **C-9. External `GET /graphs/{id}` with input schema** — so consumers can discover required inputs programmatically before executing.

---

## Suggested sequencing

Track A is ~a few days and immediately improves the evaluation experience. Track B follows once A-1 lands (SDKs are generated from the published spec). Track C delivers enterprise-grade reliability and is the deepest work.

Recommended starting points: **A-1** (publish the spec) and **B-5** (SDK generation), since both unlock everything downstream.

## Related

- Hardening / security epic: #13556

title: External API: rate limiting, key usage tracking, error mapping & hardening
author: bugattive
body:
## Summary

The public **External API** (`backend/api/external/`, mounted at `/external-api`, served under `/external-api/v1/...`) is functional but has several security, robustness, and DX gaps. This issue tracks a prioritized set of improvements. Each item below is intended to become a separate, focused PR (per the repo's split-by-concern guidance).

### Current state (for context)
- Separate FastAPI sub-app (`backend/api/external/fastapi_app.py`) mounted on the main REST app (`backend/api/rest_api.py:505`), with its own docs (`/external-api/docs`), OpenAPI schema, and Prometheus metrics (`/external-api/metrics`).
- Unified auth (`backend/api/external/middleware.py`): `X-API-Key` **or** OAuth2 Bearer token; scope checks via `APIKeyPermission`. Keys stored as salted Scrypt hashes with legacy-SHA256 auto-migration.
- Surface: `/me`, `/blocks` (+ execute), `/graphs` (create/execute/results), `/store/*`, `/integrations/*`, `/tools/*`.
- Billing (paywall + credit charging) gates block/graph execution.
- Only `backend/api/external/v1/routes_test.py` exists — integrations, tools, store, and the auth middleware itself are untested.

---

## P0 — Security & correctness

### 1. No rate limiting on the external API
Only `SecurityHeadersMiddleware` is attached (`fastapi_app.py:19`); there is no per-key throttling. `tools.py:1-8` explicitly acknowledges this gap ("should be handled separately… via API key quotas") but nothing implements it. A single valid key can hammer store endpoints (whose only stated DDoS defense is auth) or spam graph executions.
- **Proposed fix:** Redis-backed rate-limit dependency keyed on `auth.user_id` + API-key id, with per-scope tiers (cheap reads vs. execution). Use `transaction=True` for atomic increments. Return `429` with `Retry-After` and `X-RateLimit-*` headers.

### 2. `lastUsedAt` is never updated
`validate_api_key` (`backend/data/auth/api_key.py:121-150`) verifies the key but never writes `lastUsedAt`, so `APIKey.lastUsedAt` in `schema.prisma` is effectively dead for external traffic — no way to detect stale/abandoned keys or build usage analytics.
- **Proposed fix:** Debounced fire-and-forget update (write at most once/60s per key via a Redis timestamp guard to avoid a DB write per request).

### 3. Broad `except Exception -> 400` collapses failure modes
`execute_graph` (`backend/api/external/v1/routes.py:228-246`) turns not-found, validation, and internal errors all into a generic 400 (paywall is re-raised first as a workaround). This misreports 404s/500s and complicates client error handling.
- **Proposed fix:** Catch specific exceptions (`NotFoundError -> 404`, validation -> 422, unexpected -> 500) via a shared handler; stop `unicode_escape`-decoding error strings.

---

## P1 — Robustness & consistency

### 4. Move per-request work off the hot path
`list_providers` / OAuth resolution call `load_all_blocks()` on the request path with a broad `except Exception: log.warning` (`integrations.py:197-201, 264-270`) — silent failures + latency. Cache the provider capability map at startup.

### 5. Fix local/inner imports (violates "top-level imports only")
`routes.py:159` (`library_db`), `routes.py:217` (`get_user_default_team`), `integrations.py:378` (`datetime`), `integrations.py:197,213,266,272` (`os`, block registry). Hoist to module top.

### 6. Bound `page_size` on store pagination
`routes.py:348-352` validates `page >= 1` / `page_size >= 1` but has no upper cap — clients can request an arbitrarily large page. Clamp to a max (e.g. 100).

### 7. Harden credential deletion
`delete_credential` (`integrations.py:614-618`) only removes the local record — it does not revoke tokens with the provider or clean up webhooks (documented in its own docstring). Add best-effort provider revocation + webhook teardown.

### 8. Tighten OAuth completion
Scope mismatch on completion only logs a warning (`integrations.py:443-447`) instead of failing; callback-origin matching is exact-string + blanket-localhost (`integrations.py:166`). Make scope mismatch a hard error and support explicit allowlisted redirect patterns.

---

## P2 — DX, docs, observability

### 9. HTTP caching for public store endpoints
Store data is public, but `SecurityHeadersMiddleware`'s `CACHEABLE_PATHS` (`backend/api/middleware/security.py:13-51`) has no `/external-api/*` entries, so every response is `no-store`. Allow short `Cache-Control: public, max-age=…` on `/external-api/v1/store/*` (an internal result cache already exists; expose it at the HTTP layer).

### 10. Versioning headroom
Only `v1` exists with no deprecation strategy. Document a version policy and adopt a `Sunset`/`Deprecation` header convention before v2 is needed.

### 11. Test coverage
Add tests for the auth middleware (valid/invalid/expired key, bearer path, 403 on missing scope), integrations router (OAuth initiate/complete, credential CRUD, callback validation), tools router, and store pagination bounds — the largest untested surfaces.

---

## Suggested sequencing (one PR each)
1. `feat(backend): API-key rate limiting for external API` (P0-1)
2. `fix(backend): track lastUsedAt + specific error mapping on external API` (P0-2, P0-3)
3. `refactor(backend/external): hoist imports, cache provider map, bound pagination` (P1-4,5,6)
4. `fix(backend/external): harden credential deletion + OAuth completion` (P1-7,8)
5. `feat(backend/external): HTTP caching for public store endpoints` (P2-9)
6. `test(backend/external): cover auth, integrations, tools` (P2-11)

title: ExecuteCodeBlock: UnicodeEncodeError when data contains emoji (surrogate pairs in AGPT_VARIABLES env var)
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Bug

When an upstream block (e.g. NotionReadDatabaseBlock) returns data containing emoji in property values (e.g. `"Holidays 🎅🏻"`), ExecuteCodeBlock crashes **before user code runs** with:

```
UnicodeEncodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't encode characters in position 25103-25106: surrogates not allowed
```

## Root cause

The variable injection mechanism (PR #13300 / issue #13286) serializes block inputs into the `AGPT_VARIABLES` environment variable. When upstream data contains emoji encoded as UTF-16 surrogate pairs, Python 3.13's `os.environ.__setitem__` raises `UnicodeEncodeError: surrogates not allowed` at line 5 of the injected preamble.

## Fix

Sanitise or safely encode surrogate characters when building `AGPT_VARIABLES`, e.g. via `json.dumps(..., ensure_ascii=True)` or re-encoding with `surrogatepass`, so ExecuteCodeBlock can handle arbitrary Unicode data from upstream blocks.

## Workaround

Filter upstream data to exclude emoji-containing entries before it reaches ExecuteCodeBlock.

## References

* Variable injection PR: #13300
* Variable injection issue: #13286

title: New sidebar shows half-localized date format that is incorrect for every locale
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Problem

The new app sidebar displays dates in a format that appears half-localized: it mixes conventions in a way that isn't correct for any locale (see screenshot in the Discord report).

## Suggested fix

Format dates with the user's locale end-to-end, e.g. via `Intl.DateTimeFormat(undefined, ...)` / `toLocaleDateString()` with a single consistent options set, instead of hand-assembling date parts.

## Context

The new sidebar is behind a feature flag (enabled on Dev); at the time of the report only the Files page had been optimized for it, so this may be part of the broader sidebar polish track (e.g. #13474, #13513) — but there was no tracking item for the date format specifically.

## Source

Reported by Reinier in #frontend on 2026-07-04 (link attached).

title: Marketplace agent download saves as agent_{id}.json — use a human-readable filename
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Problem

Downloading an agent from the Marketplace works, but the downloaded file is named `agent_{id}.json` (a UUID), which is meaningless to the user and makes downloaded agents hard to tell apart.

## Suggested fix

Use the agent's name (slugified) in the filename, e.g. `my-agent-name.json` or `my-agent-name_v3.json` — optionally keeping the id or version as a suffix for uniqueness.

## Source

Reported by Reinier during v0.6.66 release QA on 2026-07-04 (link attached).

title: Name given during onboarding ("what should I call you?") is not used on the AutoPilot home screen
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Problem

During onboarding, the first question asks "what should I call you?". The answer is not actually used: after completing onboarding with a test account and entering "Reinier", the AutoPilot home screen greeting shows a different name (see screenshot in the Discord report).

## Repro

1. Sign up with a fresh account
2. Complete onboarding, entering a name at "what should I call you?"
3. Land on the AutoPilot home screen → greeting does not use the entered name

## Source

Reported by Reinier in #breakage on 2026-07-02 (link attached).

## Notes

* Likely same underlying issue as [REQ-20](https://linear.app/autogpt/issue/REQ-20) ("Copilot greeting displays incorrect/hardcoded name instead of user's actual name") — if confirmed, merge/dedupe.
* [SECRT-1812](https://linear.app/autogpt/issue/SECRT-1812) fixed an email-vs-username variant of this in February; this report is post-fix, about the onboarding-provided name specifically.

title: AutoPilot churns through reasoning/compaction loops on simple agent-build requests until turn budget is exhausted
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Problem

Given a fairly simple agent-build request, AutoPilot churns and churns — reasoning, fetching info, reading the builder guide, compacting, over and over — until the turn budget runs out, having accomplished only half of its goal.

Observed on Prod (2026-06-29) with default settings (the configuration all Prod users get), so this hits regular users, not just internal accounts with special model/mode settings.

## Impact

* A simple agent pair (fetch a web page → check for new content → send an email if new) eventually succeeded but consumed **\~80% of a PRO plan's daily limit**.
* Turns regularly die on budget exhaustion with the goal half-done (see Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#13398 for the error-UX side of that failure).
* The churn is on the basic agent logic, not trigger-related functionality.

## Source

Reported by Reinier in #breakage on 2026-06-29 (links attached). Earlier occurrence of the same pattern: [https://discord.com/channels/1126875755960336515/1126875756925046928/1517157809567961298](<https://discord.com/channels/1126875755960336515/1126875756925046928/1517157809567961298>)

## Notes

* One contributing factor identified at the time — misleading StoreValueBlock vs PersistInformationBlock descriptions leading AutoPilot down broken paths — was fixed in Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#13445, but the broader churn/compaction loop behavior remains.
* Related: [SECRT-1856](https://linear.app/autogpt/issue/SECRT-1856) (exhaust simpler solutions first), Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#13398 (budget-exceeded turn kill UX).

title: AutoPilot UI auto-opens internal tool output files (toolu_*.json) in artifact panel
author: Torantulino
body:
## Problem

When AutoPilot generates intermediate/internal files such as `toolu_{id}.json` (tool call output files written implicitly during execution), the artifact panel auto-opens to display them. These files have no user-facing value and should not trigger the panel.

In half-screen layouts this is particularly disruptive: the panel opening squishes the AutoPilot chat to half width, degrading the conversation experience for a file the user never asked to see.

## Expected Behavior

The artifact panel should only auto-open for files that are meaningful user-facing outputs. Internally-generated intermediate files (e.g. `toolu_*.json`) should be silently ignored by the auto-open trigger.

## Suggested Fix

Filter out files matching patterns like `toolu_*.json` (or more broadly, any file written implicitly by the tool execution harness rather than explicitly by the agent as a user-facing output) from the artifact panel's auto-open logic.

## Notes

* Not a breaking bug, but a noticeable UX regression in split/half-screen layouts
* Related context: SECRT-2263 (artifact panel auto-opens on new artifact) — the auto-open behavior introduced there doesn't currently distinguish user-facing artifacts from internal intermediate files

title: [Proposal] HeartFlow - Cognitive Engine for AutoGPT
author: yun520-1
body:
## Proposal: HeartFlow Cognitive Engine × AutoGPT

### Vision

AutoGPT gives GPT *autonomy*. HeartFlow gives it *judgment*.

### What is HeartFlow?

HeartFlow (心虫) is an **AI cognitive engine** (68 modules) that adds decision-making capability to autonomous agents:

```
Core Modules:
├── Three-Layer Memory    # CORE/LEARNED/EPHEMERAL
├── Decision Routing      # 34 rules → 8 strategies
├── U/D/A/H Field        # Cognitive health monitoring
├── Self-Healing RL      # Q-learning improvement
└── Shield Module        # Security guardrails
```

### U/D/A/H Cognitive Framework

Real-time cognitive health monitoring for autonomous agents:

```
H = 0.4·U + 0.3·D - 0.3·A

U (Unity): Agent identity consistency
D (Development): Task progression
A (Adversity): Error/resistance detection
H (Harmony): Overall agent health
```

### Benchmarks

| Task | Baseline | +HeartFlow |
|------|----------|------------|
| Logical Reasoning | 67% | **100%** |
| Decision Making | 50% | **100%** |
| Self-Correction | None | **Q-learning** |

### AutoGPT × HeartFlow

1. **Memory**: Three-layer persistence for long-term autonomy
2. **Decision Quality**: U/D/A/H cognitive metrics for agent decisions
3. **Safety**: Shield module prevents prompt injection in autonomous mode
4. **Self-improvement**: Q-learning enables continuous learning

### Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/yun520-1/mark-heartflow-skill
- DeepSeek Discussion: https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3/issues/1447

---
*HeartFlow v5.5.1 — Autonomous AI with Judgment*

title: orchestrate/SKILL.md — Mental queue for /pr-test serialization is fragile across context resets
author: vibemasshq-dev
body:
Hi team,
While auditing this repo with VibeMass I noticed the orchestrate SKILL.md relies on Claude's in-context "mental queue" to serialize /pr-test runs and prevent port conflicts and database corruption.
This pattern works within a single session but breaks silently when:

- Context window compacts mid-orchestration

- Session restarts between PR tests

- Multiple orchestrator instances run simultaneously

A lightweight mechanical guard would make this more robust without changing the workflow:

```bash
# At start of /pr-test
if [ -f /tmp/pr-test.lock ]; then
  echo "Another pr-test is running. Please wait."
  exit 1
fi
touch /tmp/pr-test.lock
trap "rm -f /tmp/pr-test.lock" EXIT
```

This could be added to the SKILL.md as a recommended pattern alongside the existing mental queue instruction.
Happy to open a PR if this direction makes sense to the team.

title: Test
author: ferhimedamine

title: Memory backend: Dakera — decay-weighted persistent memory for AutoGPT Platform agents
author: ferhimedamine
body:
## Summary

AutoGPT Platform has a memory system but it lacks importance decay — every stored memory competes equally regardless of age or relevance. This proposes **DakeraMemoryBackend** as a pluggable memory option that adds decay-weighted recall to AutoGPT Platform agents.

## Problem

AutoGPT's current memory stores accumulate everything with equal weight. As agents run over weeks and months:
1. Stale context from old tasks pollutes retrieval
2. No mechanism to "forget" low-relevance facts naturally
3. Memory quality degrades over time without manual curation

## Proposed Integration

```python
from backend.data.memory import MemoryBackend

class DakeraMemoryBackend(MemoryBackend):
    """Dakera-backed memory with decay weighting.
    
    Setup: docker run -d -p 3300:3300 -e DAKERA_API_KEY=demo ghcr.io/dakera-ai/dakera:latest
    """
    
    def __init__(self, base_url: str, api_key: str = "", agent_id: str = "autogpt"):
        from dakera import DakeraClient
        self._client = DakeraClient(base_url=base_url, api_key=api_key)
        self._agent_id = agent_id
    
    async def add(self, content: str, metadata: dict = None) -> str:
        return self._client.store_memory(
            agent_id=self._agent_id,
            content=content,
            metadata=metadata or {},
        )
    
    async def search(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list[dict]:
        response = self._client.recall(agent_id=self._agent_id, query=query, top_k=top_k)
        return [{"content": m.content, "score": m.score} for m in (response.memories if response else [])]
```

Configured via env var:
```
MEMORY_BACKEND=dakera
DAKERA_URL=http://localhost:3300
DAKERA_API_KEY=demo
```

## Decay Weighting in Practice

Dakera's half-life decay model means:
- Memories accessed recently and frequently rank highest
- Stale memories that haven't been queried in weeks fade naturally
- No manual "forget" calls required for routine cleanup

This models how human episodic memory actually works — making AutoGPT agents genuinely smarter over time, not just larger.

## Setup

```bash
docker run -d -p 3300:3300 -e DAKERA_API_KEY=demo ghcr.io/dakera-ai/dakera:latest
pip install dakera
```

Happy to open a PR implementing `DakeraMemoryBackend` as an optional environment-configured backend.

title: fix(backend/copilot): AutoPilot drops agent_json arg in edit_agent / validate_agent_graph
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Why

When AutoPilot (CoPilot) tries to edit an agent in the library, the `edit_agent` and `validate_agent_graph` tools frequently receive no `agent_json` argument — the model omits or truncates it before it ever reaches the handler. The symptom users see:

* `validate_agent_graph` → fails with the generic "your call was truncated, break it up" guidance.
* `edit_agent` → fails with `missing_agent_json` (because `agent_id` survives, so the empty-args guard doesn't fire).

This is a real, structural bug, not a parsing issue inside the tools. By the time `agent_json` is missing it was already absent in what the model emitted. Args flow straight through to the tool with no filtering (`tool_adapter._execute_tool_sync` → `base_tool.execute(..., **args)`), so the drop happens at the model/SDK boundary.

It's worst exactly in the reported scenario — editing an existing library agent — because the model must re-emit the *entire* existing graph inline, maximizing output-token pressure.

## What's causing it

1. `agent_json` **is an unconstrained** `{"type": "object"}` — no `properties`, no `additionalProperties` (`edit_agent.py:46-49`, `validate_agent.py:41-44`; same for `create_agent`/`customize_agent`/`fix_agent`). The model gets zero structure to follow and must free-form re-emit the whole graph. Under OpenRouter's constrained tool-arg decoding this kind of unconstrained object commonly collapses to `{}`, is emitted as a stringified blob, or is dropped.
2. **The SDK path strips** `required` **from the advertised schema** — `_build_input_schema` (`tool_adapter.py:436-449`) omits `required` so empty `{}` calls reach the Python handler for friendly guidance. Side effect: on the Claude-Agent-SDK path the model is never told `agent_json` is required, so nothing compels it. (The baseline OpenAI-compat path does send `required`, so the two paths differ.)
3. **The truncation guard misses partial drops** — `_make_truncating_wrapper` only fires when args is *entirely* empty (`tool_adapter.py:753`, `if not args and required_args`). For `edit_agent` the model usually still emits `agent_id`, so args is non-empty → guard skipped → reaches `_execute` → `missing_agent_json`.
4. **Re-emitting the whole graph inline, repeatedly** — the generation guide drives validate → fix → edit → dry-run → edit, each step re-passing the full `agent_json` inline (`sdk/agent_generation_guide.md:59-78`). There's an `@@agptfile:` file-reference escape hatch (`sdk/file_ref.py`) and the truncation message even suggests it, but it only expands **string** arg values — it can't be used for an `object`-typed `agent_json`.

## How (proposed fix)

**Primary (agreed):**

1. **Give** `agent_json` **a real schema** — define `properties` (`nodes`, `links`, `name`, …) and/or `additionalProperties: true` so the decoder has structure to follow and stops collapsing to `{}`. Apply consistently across `edit_agent`, `validate_agent_graph`, `create_agent`, `customize_agent`, `fix_agent`.
2. **Support a file-reference for** `agent_json` — accept an `agent_json_ref` (string) pointing at the workspace file the guide already has the model write (`write_workspace_file(filename="agent.json")`), and load it server-side. This avoids re-emitting the full graph inline at all and directly addresses the library-edit case. Being a string param, it can leverage the existing `@@agptfile:` expansion.

**Defensive (nice-to-have):**

3. Tighten the partial-drop guard in `_make_truncating_wrapper`: when a tool's `required_args` are present but missing from non-empty args, return the truncation guidance instead of the generic `missing_agent_json`.

**Deferred / not doing for now:**

4. A diff/patch-style `edit_agent` (node-level changes keyed by node id) so the model never re-emits the full graph. Deferred — higher risk of breaking existing edit flows; revisit only if 1+2 prove insufficient.

## Acceptance

* Editing a non-trivial existing library agent via AutoPilot reliably passes `agent_json` to `edit_agent`/`validate_agent_graph`.
* Regression test reproducing the partial-drop on `edit_agent` (TDD: failing test first).
* Behavior verified on the SDK/OpenRouter path (the default), not just the baseline path.

title: Smart Decision Maker / agent-mode tool loops fail on OpenAI reasoning models due to mishandled store + reasoning-item lifecycle in Responses API adapter
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Why

The Smart Decision Maker / OrchestratorBlock agent loop dies after a single tool invoc>ation on every OpenAI reasoning model. Single-shot prompts (no tool calls) work; anything that loops fails. This is already hitting users in production — see the auto-filed Sentry stubs Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#12901 and Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#13436 (and GitHub #12901, #13436).

**Confirmed in code (this repo):**

* `backend/util/llm/providers.py:445` — `_call_openai_responses()` hardcodes `store=False` on every `client.responses.create()` call. It does **not** set `include=["reasoning.encrypted_content"]` and does **not** pass `previous_response_id`.
* `backend/util/llm/providers.py:273-301` — **all** OpenAI models route through the Responses API (there is no Chat-Completions fallback in this codebase). Reasoning models (gpt-5*, o3*) emit `reasoning` items; gpt-4.1/4o do not, so only the reasoning models hit this failure.
* `backend/blocks/orchestrator.py` (`_convert_raw_response_to_dict` → `_update_conversation`) re-inlines the prior turn's `output` items verbatim on turn N+1 via `prompt.extend(converted)`. That re-sends the `rs_...` reasoning item by id. Because `store=False`, OpenAI never persisted it, so the lookup fails.

Result on the second LLM call:

```
404 — Item with id 'rs_...' not found. Items are not persisted when `store` is set to false.
      Try again with `store` set to true, or remove this item from your input.
```

(The protocol contract: for reasoning models a reasoning item produced in turn N MUST be present on turn N+1's input — either by id reference, which requires `store=true`, or inlined verbatim with its `encrypted_content`, which works with `store=false`. We currently do neither correctly.)

## Affected

* **Block:** OrchestratorBlock / Smart Decision Maker (`3b191d9f-356f-482d-8238-ba04b6d18381`), and any path using `execution_mode=built_in` against OpenAI reasoning models.
* **Models (route through Responses API + emit reasoning items):** gpt-5-2025-08-07, gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07, gpt-5-nano-2025-08-07, gpt-5-chat-latest, gpt-5.1-2025-11-13, gpt-5.2-2025-12-11, o3-mini, o3-2025-04-16.
* **Not affected:** gpt-4.1-2025-04-14, gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini (no reasoning items, so no `rs_...` item to re-inline).

## Repro

1. Build a graph with Smart Decision Maker, model = gpt-5-2025-08-07, ≥1 tool wired in.
2. Provide a prompt that requires ≥2 tool calls.
3. First tool call fires. Second turn 404s with the "reasoning item not persisted" error, killing the loop.

## How (two candidate fixes — pick one)

**A. Server-side state.** Send `store=true`, capture `response.id`, pass `previous_response_id` on the next request, and omit prior reasoning items from `input`. Cleaner and lower bandwidth, but ties conversation state to OpenAI's servers (and to the same API key/region).

**B. Client-side state.** Keep `store=false`, but when reconstructing `input` for turn N+1, walk the prior assistant `output` and inline every reasoning item (with its `encrypted_content`) immediately before the message/tool_call it preceded. Requires adding `include=["reasoning.encrypted_content"]` to the original request so the encrypted blob comes back.

## Notes / related

* `backend/blocks/test/test_orchestrator_responses_api.py` already documents a sibling Responses-API loop failure (the `400 — Invalid value: ''` role-serialization case) and is a good home for a regression test for this fix. Follow TDD: add a failing test reproducing the 404 across a tool-call → tool-result → next-turn cycle before implementing.
* Duplicate auto-filed stubs to dedupe/close once this lands: Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#12901, Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#13436.

title: BlockUnknownError: Error code: 404 - {'error': {'message': "Item with id 'rs_00c628747304a62e016a3f97f4504c81958e373bd8c23ab7eb' not found. Items are not persisted when `store` is set to false. Try again with `store` set to true, or remove this item from y
author: linear-code[bot]

title: Random per-call json_output tag busts the Anthropic prompt cache in structured-output blocks
author: sumleo
body:
### Summary

The Anthropic structured-output path embeds a fresh per-call random token into the **cached system prompt**, so the ephemeral prompt cache is invalidated on every request and can never be read back. This silently nullifies the `cache_control` caching that was deliberately added to this path.

Found via static analysis of prompt-cache anti-patterns; confirmed by reading the code on `master` at commit `150514f`.

### Root-cause trace (on `master` @ `150514f`)

1. `autogpt_platform/backend/backend/blocks/llm.py:1491-1492` — the output tag id is freshly random every call:
   ```python
   def get_collision_proof_output_tag_id(self) -> str:
       return secrets.token_hex(8)
   ```
2. `blocks/llm.py:1174-1183` — that id is interpolated into the JSON-output instructions and appended as a **system** message:
   ```python
   output_tag_id = self.get_collision_proof_output_tag_id()
   output_tag_start = f'<json_output id="{output_tag_id}">'
   if input_data.expected_format:
       sys_prompt = self.response_format_instructions(..., output_tag_start=output_tag_start)
       prompt.append({"role": "system", "content": sys_prompt})
   ```
3. `autogpt_platform/backend/backend/util/llm/providers.py:504-541` — all system messages are joined and sent as the cached system block:
   ```python
   system_messages = [p["content"] for p in messages if p["role"] == "system"]
   sysprompt = " ".join(system_messages)
   ...
   create_kwargs["system"] = [{
       "type": "text",
       "text": sysprompt,
       "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"},
   }]
   ```
   The same wrapping exists in the batch path, `_submit_anthropic_batch` (`providers.py:1108-1146`).

Because the random `output_tag_id` changes the bytes of the cached system prefix on every call, the `cache_control: {"type": "ephemeral"}` write can never be re-read.

### Impact

Anthropic prompt caching is a prefix match — any byte change in the prefix invalidates the cached entry. Every structured-output (`expected_format`) call on the Anthropic path writes a fresh ephemeral cache that is never read back, so the system prefix (the most expensive, most stable part of the request) is re-billed at the write tier (~1.25x) instead of the read tier (~0.1x) every call, including retries. This defeats the caching the team added for exactly this hot path.

### Suggested fix

The tag only needs to **match between the instruction and the `get_json_from_response` parser** within a single response — it does not need to be unique per call. So any of these preserves correctness while keeping the cached prefix stable:

- Derive it deterministically from the output spec, e.g. `hashlib.sha1(expected_format_repr).hexdigest()[:16]`, or
- Compute it once per block instance instead of once per call, or
- Keep the random tag but place it only in the (uncached) **user** turn rather than inside the cached system block.

Happy to open a PR once you confirm the preferred direction (the CLA on `autogpt_platform/` is why I'm raising this as an issue first). I confirmed the behavior by reading the code on `master` at `150514f` — would appreciate a maintainer sanity-check of the trace above.

title: Library agent shows "Scheduled" badge despite user not scheduling it (recommended_schedule_cron treated as active schedule)
author: linear-code[bot]
body:
## Summary Agents in the Library (notably Marketplace-installed agents) display a **"Scheduled"** status badge even when the current user has not scheduled them. The fleet-summary "scheduled" count is inflated for the same reason. ## Root cause The backend's per-user filtering is **correct** — the bug is on the frontend, where a creator-defined *recommendation* is treated as if it were an active schedule. **Backend (correct):** * `backend/backend/api/features/library/db.py:67-92` — `_fetch_schedule_info()` queries the scheduler with `user_id=user_id`, so only the logged-in user's schedules are returned. * `backend/backend/api/features/library/model.py:374` — `is_scheduled=bool(schedule_info and agent.agentGraphId in schedule_info)` uses that user-filtered map. ✅ **Frontend (bug):**
`frontend/src/app/(platform)/library/hooks/executionHelpers.ts:8-13` ```ts export function isAgentScheduled(agent: { is_scheduled?: boolean; recommended_schedule_cron?: string | null; }): boolean { return !!agent.is_scheduled || !!agent.recommended_schedule_cron; } ``` The `|| !!agent.recommended_schedule_cron` clause is the culprit. `recommended_schedule_cron` comes from `agent.AgentGraph.recommendedScheduleCron` (`model.py:373`) — a property of the **graph itself**, set once by the agent's creator as a *suggestion*. It is shared by every user who installs the marketplace agent and does NOT indicate that the current user scheduled anything. As a result, any marketplace agent whose creator set a recommended cron shows the "Scheduled" badge for **every** user who has it in their library, even though no one scheduled it. This single predicate drives: * the status badge — `frontend/src/app/(platform)/library/hooks/useAgentStatus.ts:68` * the fleet-summary "scheduled" count — `frontend/src/app/(platform)/library/hooks/useAgentStatus.ts:200` (The predicate's own comment notes it is the shared scheduled-check used by the fleet summary, sitrep, status-map, and the list filter — all of them inherit the bug.) ## Steps to reproduce 1. Add a Marketplace agent whose creator defined a `recommendedScheduleCron` to your library. 2. Do **not** create a schedule for it. 3. Observe the Library card shows the "Scheduled" status badge (and it is counted under "scheduled" in the fleet summary). ## Expected behavior The "Scheduled" status should reflect only the current user's actual schedules (`is_scheduled`). A `recommended_schedule_cron` should drive a distinct affordance (e.g. a "schedule this?" suggestion), never the "Scheduled" status. ## Proposed fix Change `isAgentScheduled()` to rely on `is_scheduled` only: ```ts export function isAgentScheduled(agent: { is_scheduled?: boolean }): boolean { return !!agent.is_scheduled; } ``` Sanity-check the other call sites that funnel through this predicate (fleet summary, sitrep, status-map, list filter) to confirm they want active-schedule semantics. Add a regression test (an agent with `recommended_schedule_cron` set but `is_scheduled` false should not be "scheduled"). ## Notes * No existing Linear ticket covers this. Related context: Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#8779 (defines the "Scheduled" agent status), Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT#10800 (Scheduled Agents Visibility, canceled).
